heading: Unauthorized employment and disposition of lapsed appropriations
label: 1916 Unauthorized employment and disposition of lapsed appropriations
notes: Historical and Revision NotesDerivationU.S. CodeRevised Statutes andStatutes at Large (less so much as relates to removal).5 U.S.C. 47(less so much as relates to removal), .Aug. 23, 1912, ch. 350, § 537 Stat. 414 (2d sentence, less so much as relates to removal).5 U.S.C. 50The statement of the acts prohibited is supplied from , , as amended , §§ 6, 8, , and , , which is codified in sections 3103 and 5501 of title 5, United States Code.section 4 of the Act of Aug. 5, 1882, ch. 38922 Stat. 255June 22, 1906, ch. 351434 Stat. 449Sept. 23, 1950, ch. 1010, § 764 Stat. 986The words “upon conviction thereof” are omitted as unnecessary because punishment can be imposed only after conviction.

children.text: violates the provision of that an individual may be employed in the civil service in an Executive department at the seat of Government only for services actually rendered in connection with and for the purposes of the appropriation from which he is paid; orsection 3103 of title 5
violates the provision of that money accruing from lapsed salaries or from unused appropriations for salaries shall be covered into the Treasury of the United States;section 5501 of title 5
